title: geranium tuberosum subsp. malviflorum
intro: Geranium tuberosum subsp. malviflorum is a lesser-known species of geranium, also known as the sweet-scented cranesbill, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which contain geraniol and other compounds, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its potential in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The herbal preparations of Geranium tuberosum subsp. malviflorum include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
